originalTitle: Djibouti's president wins unprecedented sixth term with 97.8% of vote
title: Djibouti's president wins unprecedented sixth term with 97.8% of vote
content: Ismail Omar Guelleh, 78, has extended his already 27-year rule in a poll boycotted by the main opposition candidates.

originalTitle: Ukraine and Russia accuse each other of breaching Easter ceasefire
title: Ukraine and Russia accuse each other of breaching Easter ceasefire
content: The warring sides trade barbs over truce as Kremlin says Russia will not extend it unless Kyiv agrees to its terms.

originalTitle: After Iran talks falter, the big question is 'what happens next?'
title: Big Countries Try to Make Friends, But It's Hard!
content:
Hello, Friends!

Hi everyone! It's your friendly news reporter from Africa, here to share a story from far away. Today, we're talking about two very important countries and how they tried to become better friends. It's a bit like when you and your friend have a disagreement and then try to talk about it. Let's find out what happened!

Who Are Iran and the USA?

Imagine our big, beautiful Earth. There are so many countries on it, all with different people, cultures, and stories! Today, we're going to talk about two of them: a country called Iran and a country called the USA. Iran is a country in a part of the world called the Middle East. It has very old cities, amazing stories, and beautiful carpets. It's home to many friendly people! The USA, which stands for the United States of America, is a very big country far away, across a giant ocean. It has tall buildings, famous landmarks like the Statue of Liberty, and many different kinds of people. From sunny beaches to snowy mountains, it's a huge place! These two countries are very important. What they do can affect people all over the world, just like when you make a choice, it can affect your family or friends. **Did you know?** The USA has a very famous statue called the Statue of Liberty! It is a symbol of freedom and friendship.

What Are "Talks"?

Sometimes, when friends have a disagreement or a problem, what do they do? They talk, right? They sit down and talk about what's bothering them. They try to listen and understand each other. Well, countries do that too! When we say "talks," it means that important people, like leaders or special helpers, from different countries sit together. They discuss things. They try to find ways to get along better. They try to solve big problems. It's like when you and your friend talk about who gets to play with a toy first. You talk to find a fair way so everyone can be happy. **Did you know?** Talking helps people understand each other better. It's always better to talk calmly than to shout or stay quiet when you have a problem!

When Friends Don't Agree

For a very, very long time, for 47 years, Iran and the USA have not been the best of friends. That's even longer than your mom or dad has been alive! Imagine that a whole lifetime! They have had many disagreements and have been upset with each other. This is what we call "hostility." It means they haven't been happy with each other for many, many years. Sometimes, countries don't agree on important things, and it can make them feel upset and worried with each other. It's a bit like two friends who stopped playing together a long, long time ago and haven't talked properly since. But deep down, maybe they both wish things were better. **Did you know?** 47 years is a long time. Imagine if you had to wait 47 years for your next birthday cake! It’s important to try and solve problems sooner than that!

What Happened in the Big Talk?

Because they want to be better friends and solve their problems, some very important people from Iran and the USA decided to have a big talk. They wanted to try and fix things. They sat down together for a very, very long time. They talked for 21 hours! That's almost a whole day and night without much sleep! They talked and talked and talked. They wanted to see if they could finally stop being upset with each other. They hoped to find a way to become friends again, or at least to be friendly and peaceful. Everyone around the world watched to see what would happen. Would they shake hands and smile, showing they had found a solution? But after all that talking, they couldn't agree on everything. They tried very hard, but they still have some problems to sort out. It was a bit sad because everyone hoped they would find all the answers in just one talk. It means the problems are very big and tricky. **Did you know?** Even grown-ups need to take breaks when they talk for a long time! Talking for 21 hours is a lot of effort!

Why Is It Hard to Agree?

Imagine you and your friend both want to play different games. One wants to play football, the other wants to draw a picture. It's hard to do both at the same time, right? You might need to take turns, or find a new game you both like. For countries, it's a bit like that, but much bigger and more serious! They have different ideas about how things should be in the world. They have different rules and different needs. They might even remember old disagreements that make it hard to trust each other right away. Iran wants some things to happen in the world, and the USA wants other things. When their wishes are very different, and they've not been friendly for a long time, it's very hard to find a way that makes everyone happy at once. It's like trying to share one small cookie between two people who both want the whole cookie! Sometimes, you need to find a bigger cookie, or even bake a new one together! Sometimes, it takes many, many talks to find a solution. It's like building a tall tower. You don't build it in one go! You place one brick, then another, slowly and carefully. Patience is key!

What Happens Next?

So, the talks ended, and they didn't completely agree. Does this mean they will never be friends? No! It means they need to keep trying. They might talk again another day. Maybe they will try to find smaller things they can agree on first, and then work up to the bigger things. It’s like eating a big meal, you eat it one bite at a time! It's like when you try to learn a new trick with your toy. If you don't get it right the first time, you don't give up! You try again later, or you ask for help. Talking is always the first step. Even if it doesn't work perfectly right away, it shows that people want to try and make things better. **Did you know?** Many countries around the world work together in big groups, like a big team, to help each other and solve problems peacefully. This is called diplomacy!

Why Talking is Always Good

Even though Iran and the USA didn't fix everything in just 21 hours of talking, it was still very good that they talked. Why? Because talking helps people understand each other. It helps them listen to each other's worries and hopes. It shows they care enough to try and find a peaceful way forward. In our homes, in our schools, and between countries, talking is super important. It helps us live together peacefully, sharing our world with kindness. Let's hope Iran and the USA will keep talking. Maybe one day, they will find a way to be good neighbors and friendly countries. And remember, you can always make a difference by talking kindly and listening to your friends too!
summary: Important people from Iran and the USA talked for a very long time to try and become friends after many years, but they couldn't agree on everything yet.
whyMatters: It's important because when big countries don't get along, it can make things difficult for everyone in the world. When they keep talking, it helps to make the world a safer and happier place for all of us, even kids in Africa!

originalTitle: Asha Bhosle: The sound of Bollywood dies aged 92
title: Asha Bhosle: The sound of Bollywood dies aged 92
content: Her infectious voice got fans dancing and singing, becoming the soundtrack for generations of Indians.

title: What is going on with Garnacho?

originalTitle: Union Berlin appoint first female boss in Europe's top men's leagues
title: Union Berlin appoint first female boss in Europe's top men's leagues
content: Marie-Louise Eta becomes the first woman appointed to manage a men's team in one of Europe's top five leagues as she is named interim head coach of Bundesliga side Union Berlin.

originalTitle: I like proving the doubters wrong, says England's Blindkilde Brown
title: I like proving the doubters wrong, says England's Blindkilde Brown
content: England midfielder Laura Blindkilde Brown says she "likes proving people wrong" after becoming a regular starter at Manchester City this season.
